from source import find_in_sorted

def test_find_in_sorted_missing_element_causes_recursion_error():
    """
    The buggy implementation recurses infinitely when the search range
    reduces to a single element and the target is greater than that
    element.  A simple case is an array of two elements where the
    target is not present and lies in the upper half.
    """
    arr = [1, 2]
    target = 3  # Not in the array, lies in the upper half
    # On the buggy code this will raise RecursionError,
    # on the fixed code it should return -1.
    assert find_in_sorted(arr, target) == -1, (
        "find_in_sorted should return -1 for a missing target"
    )
